2017-10-01.title: Skating Through Tel Aviv's Empty Highways on Yom Kippur
2017-10-01.abstract: Tel Aviv's roads and highways are devoid of traffic on Yom Kippur, the holiest day of the Jewish year. While many people are at synagogues and at home with family, others explore the quiet on bikes and skateboards.

2017-10-01.title: Even in Death, the Spy Kim Philby Serves the Kremlin’s Purposes
2017-10-01.abstract: A flurry of tributes to Kim Philby, the British spy who defected to Moscow, signals the growing influence of Russia’s military and security services.

2017-10-01.title: Catalonia’s Independence Vote Descends Into Chaos and Clashes
2017-10-01.abstract: Hundreds were injured as riot police officers, some firing rubber bullets, dispersed voters, Catalan officials said. Dozens of police officers were also hurt.

2017-10-01.title: Captured ISIS Fighters’ Refrain: ‘I Was Only a Cook’
2017-10-01.abstract: Among thousands of civilians fleeing to Kirkuk after an Iraqi offensive are hundreds of Islamic State fighters. Many claim they were noncombatants.

2017-10-01.title: Afghan Teenagers Recruited in Iran to Fight in Syria, Group Says
2017-10-01.abstract: Human Rights Watch said it had verified the deaths of eight Afghan boys who died fighting in Syria, where Iran backs the government.

2017-10-02.abstract: Jagmeet Singh, a newcomer to federal politics, took over leadership of Canada’s New Democratic Party.

2017-10-03.title: Growing Up in Prison

2017-10-04.abstract: Pressed by lawmakers, Defense Secretary Jim Mattis reiterated his view that the Iran accord is working, a quiet break with President Trump.

2017-10-04.abstract: The Royal Air Force tracked the flight, which originated in Kaunas, Lithuania, because of a security threat that the airline later said was most likely a hoax.

2017-10-04.abstract: Prime Minister Malcolm Turnbull is set to push for a major expansion of a facial recognition database. Privacy advocates are wary.

2017-10-04.abstract: The British prime minister, who needed a commanding speech to quell a warring party, lost her voice and was handed a mock job dismissal note.

2017-10-04.abstract: A court also heard that videos of women being tortured were found on a hard drive belonging to Peter Madsen, who has been charged in the journalist’s death.

2017-10-05.abstract: Despite the pressure, Parliament passed a bill to bar Canadians from business dealings with foreigners who have committed human rights violations.

2017-10-05.abstract: This week’s newsletter explores Australia’s sense of when to strictly abide by the rules, and when to ignore, bend or change them.

2017-10-06.abstract: Officials are facing pressure to decide whether to charge the man with sparse evidence or hold him in indefinite wartime detention, which the military has not done since 2008.

2017-10-06.abstract: The International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons was honored for a new treaty signed by more than 50 countries, none of them nuclear powers.

2017-10-07.abstract: The journalist, Edgar Daniel Esqueda Castro, a photographer for online publications, had previously reported being threatened by the police.

2017-10-07.abstract: The remote Haitian island of Île-à-Vache was devastated by Hurricane Matthew in October 2016. Some residents have been able to rebuild their homes. For others, it is taking longer.

2017-10-07.abstract: In 1990, 47 women took to the road to demand the right to drive. They paid dearly for it, but they have lived to see the end of the ban.

2017-10-07.abstract: U.S. officials appear powerless to secure their release, and the dozen detainees may be bargaining chips in Turkey’s attempts to have Fethullah Gulen extradited.

2017-10-08.abstract: The explosion in Accra, Ghana’s capital, which injured at least 132 others, sent up giant balls of fire that lit up the night sky.

2017-10-08.abstract: Farmers have long played a defining role in Catalonian identity. Now they are playing a big role in the independence movement, by using their tractors.

2017-10-09.abstract: Fifty years after the revolutionary leader was shot to death in Bolivia, his symbolic power remains.

2017-10-09.abstract: The country is facing a humanitarian crisis that is threatening the lives, health and futures of its young people.

2017-10-09.abstract: The sister, Kim Yo-jong, who is the only one of Mr. Kim’s siblings active in his government, was made an alternate member of the Politburo.

2017-10-10.abstract: A Trump administration decision to impose a nearly 300 percent tariff on imports of a new plane threatens thousands of jobs and the peace process.

2017-10-10.abstract: It was the third case in which a court found the Tokyo Electric Power Company negligent in not preventing the meltdowns set off by an earthquake and tsunami in March 2011.

2017-10-10.abstract: The government revealed that one of its national security contractors was extensively hacked last year.

2017-10-18.abstract: Thousands of Rohingya fleeing violence have crossed the border to Bangladesh, but their journey is far from over.

2017-10-19.abstract: Kurdish and Iraqi forces, both armed by the Americans, fought each other in Kirkuk. Analysts think Iran played a key role in the dispute’s resolution.

2017-10-19.abstract: This week’s newsletter digs into discussion and events with The New York Times in Australia.

2017-10-19.abstract: A minor party’s support has put Ms. Ardern, the Labour standard-bearer, in line to be the country’s youngest prime minister in more than 150 years.

2017-10-19.abstract: Carles Puigdemont, Catalonia’s leader, said in a letter to the Spanish prime minister that the regional Parliament could vote to break away if Madrid tried to seize administrative control.

2017-10-20.abstract: Extremely tight security during an important Communist Party meeting reflects President Xi Jinping’s vision of an all-powerful government.

2017-10-20.abstract: The decision to restart two stalled projects reverses a campaign promise by President Moon Jae-in and disappoints supporters seeking a phaseout of nuclear power.

2017-10-20.abstract: On Friday, Holden employees bid farewell to Australia’s last car factory, wondering if the government could have done more to save it.

2017-10-21.abstract: Nigerien officials have said that a convoy gave chase to Islamic militants who later returned to ambush them, but American troops say they didn’t pursue the militants before the attack.

2017-10-21.abstract: Mr. Xi has promised a new “China dream” of prosperity. But in the nation’s hinterlands, locals say there’s a stark disconnect between the bright promises and their hardscrabble reality.

2017-10-22.abstract: The two American allies are vying for international support days after Iraq drove Kurdish fighters from almost all contested areas.

2017-10-22.abstract: A look at what the Islamic State controlled at different stages of its military dominance.

2017-10-22.abstract: A storm of criticism had greeted the appointment of the Zimbabwe’s leader this past week.

2017-10-24.abstract: Claims that atrocities were staged, along with stark hate speech against Rohingya Muslims, are blaring out from social media and official statements.

2017-10-24.abstract: Joshua Wong and Nathan Law, key figures in the democracy movement, are challenging the sentences that led allies to call them political prisoners.

2017-10-25.abstract: The Communist Party has added the president’s name and ideas to its constitution, solidifying his position as China’s most powerful leader in decades.

2017-10-25.abstract: The lineup signals a new era of strongman politics in China, with speculation that Mr. Xi will break with tradition and rule beyond his second term.

2017-10-25.title: These Seven Men Now Run China
2017-10-25.abstract: President Xi Jinping announced the new lineup for China’s Politburo Standing Committee, the body that will determine policy for the next five years.

2017-10-25.title: China’s ‘Chairman of Everything’: Behind Xi Jinping’s Many Titles
2017-10-25.abstract: Mr. Xi has used a bureaucrat’s savvy to cement his control, inventing titles and institutions.

2017-10-26.title: Turkish Court Frees Human Rights Workers in Surprising Move
2017-10-26.abstract: Turkey released eight people, including two foreign citizens, who had been charged with terrorism, a rare softening in its yearlong coup prosecutions.

2017-10-26.title: Bomb Wounds Ukrainian Politician as Assassination Plots Mount
2017-10-26.abstract: The lawmaker was known for pugnacious stands on the war in eastern Ukraine and heated attacks on Russia. His bodyguard was killed, and a commentator was wounded.

2017-10-26.title: President Temer of Brazil Dodges Corruption Prosecution, Again
2017-10-26.abstract: Before the vote on whether he should stand trial, the president made major policy concessions and pledged money for legislators’ districts.

2017-10-26.title: Australia Battles Amazon, With Populist Defiance
2017-10-26.abstract: This week’s newsletter examines the urge to resist outsiders, from the bookish left and the populist right.

2017-10-26.title: To Hold Off the Right, New Dutch Coalition Partly Embraces It
2017-10-26.abstract: The new government of the Netherlands, one of Europe’s most progressive countries, is taking conservative stances on immigration and national identity.

2017-10-27.title: U.S. Soldiers Were Separated From Unit in Niger Ambush, Officials Say
2017-10-27.abstract: New details have changed the timeline to the attack as Pentagon officials investigate the ambush that left four Americans and five Nigeriens dead.

2017-10-27.title: In Rare Gesture, North Says It Will Return South Korean Fishing Boat
2017-10-27.abstract: Though tensions have been high, North Korea says it will send home a South Korean fishing crew captured after its boat crossed the sea border.

2017-10-27.title: Australia Deputy Leader Among 5 to Lose Seats Over Citizenship
2017-10-27.abstract: The high court ruled that Barnaby Joyce and other lawmakers had been ineligible to run for Parliament because they held dual citizenship.

2017-10-27.title: Australian Government Rejects Move for Indigenous ‘Recognition’
2017-10-27.abstract: The government said a proposal to create body representing Indigenous people would have failed if brought to a vote in a national referendum.

2017-10-27.title: Mattis Visit to DMZ Highlights the North Korean Threat to Seoul
2017-10-27.abstract: Gazing over the border with his South Korean counterpart, Defense Secretary Jim Mattis was reminded of the devastating power in range of the capital.

2017-10-28.title: In Seoul, Mattis Accuses North Korea of ‘Outlaw Behavior’
2017-10-28.abstract: Defense Secretary Jim Mattis said that Pyongyang was accelerating the threat of nuclear war through missile tests and that the United States would protect its allies.

2017-10-28.title: One Convicted, One Cleared: Signs of Trouble in Indonesia Courts
2017-10-28.abstract: The legal system is coming under renewed scrutiny after rulings in two high-profile cases that alarmed both Westerners and local anticorruption groups.

2017-10-28.title: Iceland Goes to Polls Amid Scandals, Disgust and Distrust
2017-10-28.abstract: As Iceland votes in parliamentary elections on Saturday, a disgraced former prime minister is among those competing to govern.

2017-10-28.title: 796 Irish Children Vanished. Why?
2017-10-28.abstract: For decades, the bones of children lay near a home for unmarried mothers and their babies. It took an amateur historian and the horrific memories of those who had lived there to bring the tragedy to light.

2017-10-28.title: Public Shaming and Even Prison for Plastic Bag Use in Rwanda
2017-10-28.abstract: In Rwanda, it is illegal to import, produce, use or sell plastic bags, and violators face stiff punishment. But the zero tolerance policy appears to be paying off: The streets are spotless.

2017-10-29.title: Mali’s Desert Elephants, on Edge of Annihilation, Get a Fighting Chance
2017-10-29.abstract: With its elephants being slaughtered in staggering numbers, Mali created an armed brigade to defend them. The force faces threats not only from poachers but also terrorists and bandits.

2017-10-29.title: Iceland’s Independence Party Retains Most Seats After Election
2017-10-29.abstract: But the conservative party, led by Prime Minister Bjarni Benediktsson, emerged somewhat weakened, losing five seats.

2017-10-29.title: Spain Is a Collection of Glued Regions. Or Maybe Not So Glued.
2017-10-29.abstract: Catalonia’s independence declaration is illegal under Spain’s Constitution. What rights do Catalonia and Spain’s 16 other autonomous regions enjoy?

2017-10-29.title: Mexico’s Day of the Dead Parade Pays Tribute to Quake Victims
2017-10-29.abstract: The parade also celebrated the rescuers who helped pull people from the rubble of collapsed buildings after two earthquakes in two weeks in September.

2017-10-29.title: Afghan Taliban Awash in Heroin Cash, a Troubling Turn for War
2017-10-29.abstract: The Taliban are increasingly refining their own opium, rather than exporting its ingredients, lifting their profits and giving them less reason to talk.

2017-10-30.title: Kurdish Leader Quits, Latest Fallout From Much-Criticized Independence Vote
2017-10-30.abstract: Massoud Barzani had pushed for the independence referendum that many Kurdish leaders now see as a catastrophic blunder.

2017-10-30.title: Out of Sixth Grade, and Straight to the Factory She Died In
2017-10-30.abstract: At least three underage girls were caught in the blaze at an Indonesian fireworks plant, reflecting struggles with workplace safety and child welfare.

2017-10-30.title: Damage in Deserted Barbuda
2017-10-30.abstract: Hurricane Irma forced the entire island of Barbuda to evacuate in September. Prime Minister Gaston Browne explains what's still keeping many of its 1,700 residents from their homes.

2017-10-30.title: France, Land of Croissants, Finds Butter Vanishing From Shelves
2017-10-30.abstract: A price spike has led to sporadic shortages in one of the world’s most butter-loving countries, prompting both mock panic and real anxiety.

2017-10-30.title: Catalonia Leader Turns Up in Belgium After Spain Seeks Prosecution
2017-10-30.abstract: Carles Puigdemont, the deposed Catalan leader, arrived amid speculation that he may seek asylum or try to form a government in exile

2017-10-31.title: Afghan War Data, Once Public, Is Censored in U.S. Military Report
2017-10-31.abstract: Key figures on the progress of local security forces, compiled by the government’s spending watchdog, were classified only once before in nine years.

2017-10-31.title: Read the Latest Report by the Watchdog for Afghanistan Reconstruction
2017-10-31.abstract: The American military command in Afghanistan has redacted key figures about the growth and progress of local security forces from this report by the special inspector general for Afghanistan reconstruction.

2017-10-31.title: U.S. Pledges $60 Million for Antiterrorism Force in Africa
2017-10-31.abstract: The pledge will help finance a 5,000-troop force from five African nations including Niger, where four American soldiers were killed in early October.

2017-10-31.title: A Beer Festival in China Has German Roots, but No Lederhosen
2017-10-31.abstract: Thousands attended the Qingdao International Beer Festival this summer in China. If it looks like Oktoberfest, there is a good reason.

2017-10-31.title: How Catalan Separatists Pitched Independence
2017-10-31.abstract: Fueled by economic discontent and a long-standing schism with Spain's conservative central government, Catalonia's separatists have tried pitching independent statehood as a fight for democracy itself.
